evals.prompt: I have a folder of LiveKit call transcript JSON files and I need to extract from each one: the caller's name (if mentioned), a one-sentence summary of their issue, whether the issue was resolved, and any follow-up action items. Some calls might not mention the caller's name so that should be optional. Output as JSON.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that loads LiveKit JSON transcripts, defines a Pydantic model with Optional caller_name, extracts structured fields, handles missing data gracefully, and writes results to JSON.

assertions.name: uses_pydantic_model
assertions.description: Defines a Pydantic BaseModel for the output structure

assertions.name: has_optional_fields
assertions.description: Uses Optional for caller_name since it may not be present

assertions.name: loads_livekit_transcripts
assertions.description: Reads JSON files and extracts text from segments array

assertions.name: outputs_json
assertions.description: Writes extracted data to a JSON file

assertions.name: handles_batch
assertions.description: Processes multiple transcript files, not just one

evals.prompt: I get vendor invoices as plain text emails and I need to extract: vendor name, invoice number, date, line items (each with description, quantity, unit price, total), subtotal, tax, and grand total. Some invoices have 2 line items, some have 20. I have about 30 already manually extracted that I can use as training data.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that defines a nested Pydantic model (Invoice with list of LineItems), loads training data, evaluates baseline, optimizes with BootstrapFewShot, and saves the optimized parser.

assertions.name: uses_nested_pydantic
assertions.description: Defines Invoice model with nested list[LineItem] for variable-length line items

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature class for the extraction task

assertions.name: loads_training_data
assertions.description: Shows how to load the 30 labeled examples

assertions.name: includes_evaluation
assertions.description: Evaluates extraction quality with a metric

assertions.name: includes_optimization
assertions.description: Uses a DSPy optimizer to improve accuracy

evals.prompt: I have a CSV with a 'notes' column containing free-text doctor visit notes. I need to extract: patient complaints (list), diagnosis, prescribed medications (list with name and dosage), and follow-up date. Some notes are really messy and abbreviated. I want to validate that medications have both a name and dosage.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that reads CSV, defines Pydantic models with validation, uses dspy.Suggest or Assert to validate medication fields, processes batch, handles messy input gracefully.

assertions.name: uses_pydantic_model
assertions.description: Defines structured output model for medical note fields

assertions.name: has_list_fields
assertions.description: Uses list types for complaints and medications

assertions.name: includes_validation
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Suggest or dspy.Assert to validate medication has name and dosage

assertions.name: loads_csv
assertions.description: Reads from the CSV file with pandas or csv module

assertions.name: handles_batch
assertions.description: Processes all rows, not just a single example
